texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 7.8 System Malfunction and Gas Control Failure Flowchart Table
texts.text: 7.8 System Malfunction and Gas Control Failure Flowchart Table

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Error Log includes technical alarms and other error conditions reported by the system.
texts.text: The Error Log includes technical alarms and other error conditions reported by the system.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: A technical alarm (equipment alarm), as apposed to a parameter alarm, is an alarm condition that exists whether or not a patient is connected to the machine. Technical alarms include:
texts.text: A technical alarm (equipment alarm), as apposed to a parameter alarm, is an alarm condition that exists whether or not a patient is connected to the machine. Technical alarms include:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Failed state alarms - internal problem prevents normal operation
texts.text: Failed state alarms - internal problem prevents normal operation

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Alarms that do not fit into any particular category but are technical in nature are referred to as a Status alarms in this table.
texts.text: Alarms that do not fit into any particular category but are technical in nature are referred to as a Status alarms in this table.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Log entries may be preceded with the subsystem that reported the error condition (example: 'ACB: +12.0V H AMPS ALT O2'). The Error Log entry listed below does not include the subsystem prefix that reported the alarm (ACB:).
texts.text: Log entries may be preceded with the subsystem that reported the error condition (example: 'ACB: +12.0V H AMPS ALT O2'). The Error Log entry listed below does not include the subsystem prefix that reported the alarm (ACB:).

table_cells.text: Turn power Off and On for self tests
System has been operating for longer than 12 hours without a power up self test.
At next available time, set the On/Standby switch from the On to Off, then set back to On.
Internal failure. System may shut down.
Check the AC-DC module connection. Check AC-DC module status in Service Application. If problem continues, replace the AC-DC power supply board.

table_cells.text: ACB +12.5 V (10 VA) value is between 12.125 V and 13.125 V.
ACB +12.5 V (10 VA) value is between 12.125 V and 13.125 V.
AC clock frequency > 1.1 times of expected clock frequency or < 0.9 times of expected clock frequency
The system is in the Therapy state
Reboot system. If problem continues, replace the ACB.
After establishing initial communication, the Display Computer does not receive any messages from Anesthesia Computer for 10 seconds.
Reboot System. If problem continues, replace the ACB.
A CPU instruction Test Failure occurs
The system is in the Therapy state
Reboot System. If problem continues, replace the ACB.
The Anesthesia Computer receives no system state messages from the Display Computer for 10 seconds.

Reboot System. If problem continues, check the ACB to FIB communication LED's (or SIB communication LED's). If the RCV and XMT (or TXD and RXD) LED's indicate activity, check DU cable connections, replace Display Controller PCB if problem continues. If the RCV and XMT (or TXD and RXD) LED's indicate no activity, check the ACB connection, replace the ACB if problem continues.
Read/Write failure or CRC failure of the EEPROM located on the ACB.
Reboot System. If problem continues, replace the
CRC Failure occurs in the code space.
Reboot System. If problem continues, replace the ACB.
